violations.text: At the time of inspection, an allergen awareness statement was not observed visible to consumers at the point of sale, menu board, or on new menus. Person in charge is to provide an allergy awareness statement that is visible to consumers on the menu and menu boards, as well as on-line and ordering service menus. New Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026

At the time of inspection, an employee was observed prepping vegetables at a table in the dining room. PIC made aware any food items being prepped for food service are to be prepared in an approved food prep area (kitchen). Food prep in the dining room was discontinued immediately at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026

In use utensils (knives) were observed stored between prep counter/equipment surfaces. Knives are to be stored in a way to prevent contamination of the food contact surfaces. Knives were removed at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026

Raw food items (chicken, beef, shrimp) held in refrigeration were observed not stored properly to prevent cross-contact/cross-contamination in the prep unit and the glass door cooler. Food items were rearranged for proper storage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (food slicer and meat tumbler/marinade machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Food processing units taken out of service until properly cleaned and sanitized prior to next use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

At the time of inspection, dry goods ingredients were observed stored open and not in storage containers to prevent possible contamination of product. PIC is to ensure food items are protected by proper storage preventing possible contamination from the premises. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

A knife used at the prep unit for cutting vegetables was observed stored between the side of the prep unit and the refrigeration unit next to it. In-Use utensils are to be stored properly in a way to prevent contamination of the food contact surface. Knife was removed and brought to the dish area at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Bowl(s) removed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

)SINGLE-SERVICE and SINGLE-USE ARTICLES shall be stored/kept in the original protective PACKAGE or stored by using other means that afford protection from contamination until used (to be stored inverted on a clean surface). Single-service articles were rearranged for proper storage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

The following equipment was observed in need of repair/adjustment.. - the food tumbler/marinade machine drum was observed to have a joint sealed with a paste material (per the PIC made from dry ingredients). PIC is to ensure the drum is properly repaired to ensure proper washing and sanitizing of equipment can occur. PIC is to discontinue use of machine until properly repaired. - A wire strainer/sifter was observed to have frayed wires. Strainer was voluntarily disposed of by the PIC at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

The following equipment non-food contact surfaces were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris... - cookline equipment (sides between equipment) - refrigeration surfaces internal/external - storage shelving racks in dish area and in the walk-in cooler - food processing equipment control buttons/switches Equipment is to be cleaned at a frequency necessary to keep them clean and to preclude the buildup of soil and debris. (Cleaning of equipment began by staff at the time of inspection.) New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

The floor/walls under and behind equipment along the cookline area were observed to have an accumulation of grease and debris. PIC is to ensure that the physical facilities are cleaned at a frequency necessary to keep them clean. (Cleaning by staff began at the time of inspection.) New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety, that is licensed/registered with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection, at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Owner Food Safety Manager License observed to have expired March, 2024. Per the Owner, Food Safety Manager License Application to be submitted same day. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024

At the time of inspection, food containers were used for food prep were observed stored in contact with food items (Stacked on food). Food containers are to be stored separate and not in contact with other food items to prevent possible contamination of foods. Food containers were removed and separated at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Bowls were removed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024

At the time of inspection, the following equipment was observed in poor repair... - Several wire strainers with broken/frayed wires. - Knife with blade chipped/gouged (Knife to be sharpened or discarded) - Knife handle with tape (tape is not easily cleanable) Equipment is to be maintained in good repair and/or discarded if no longer repairable (Items disposed of at the time of inspection.) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following were observed to have an accumulation of food/soil residue... - Refrigeration/Freezers (internal/external surfaces, gaskets, shelving) - Dry food storage containers - Facility storage shelving - Walk-In Cooler Fan Shrouds Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues. New Violation. Correct By: 06/01/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap (Currently equipped with an AIR BREAK). A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2024
